# git-chunker

> CLI that splits pending git changes into multiple commits (by max files
> and/or max size per commit) and optionally pushes after each one, so every
> push stays under SCM push size limits and per-push scan/hook timeouts.
> Works as a git subcommand: `git chunker`. Non-interactive, idempotent, safe
> to rerun after failures (already-committed chunks are skipped).

Fixes errors like:
- "remote: fatal: pack exceeds maximum allowed size" (GitHub)
- "remote: GitLab: Push size limit exceeded"
- "error: RPC failed; HTTP 413" / "HTTP 500"
- "error VS403500: size of your push exceeds the limit" (Azure DevOps)
- "remote: pre-receive hook declined" (timed-out server-side scan)

Install: `npm i -g git-chunker` | `brew install jishnuteegala/tap/git-chunker` |
`winget install jishnuteegala.git-chunker` | `go install github.com/jishnuteegala/git-chunker@latest`

Common commands:
- Preview plan (JSON): `git chunker --max-size 50M --dry-run --json`
- Commit in chunks:    `git chunker --max-files 100 --max-size 50M -m "import assets"`
- Commit + push each:  `git chunker --max-size 50M --push --retries 3 --log push.log`

Behaviour notes:
- At least one of --max-files / --max-size is required.
- Progress goes to stderr; --dry-run plan goes to stdout.
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 runtime error, 2 usage error.
- On push failure after retries, committed work is preserved; rerunning the
  same command resumes (unpushed commits ride along with the next push).
- A single file larger than --max-size still gets its own commit (files
  cannot be split; use Git LFS for files over the platform hard limit).

Docs: https://github.com/jishnuteegala/git-chunker
